item.title: The U.S. and China Have a Common Foe. Hint: It’s Not the U.S.S.R.
item.description: A new level of global disorder will face the American and Chinese presidents at their summit. Like Mao and Nixon, they face a common threat.

item.title: Profoundly Autistic People Deserve Better Than Pseudoscience
item.description: Study after study has called facilitated communication into question.

item.title: This Is the Biggest Culprit for High Health Care Spending
item.description: Prices at hospitals have grown faster than prices in virtually any other sector of the economy.

item.title: Does This Feel Like a ‘Catholic Moment’?
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion guest essay by Christine Emba about a possible Catholic revival. Also: A woman’s face.

item.title: Trump Is Losing Normie Republicans
item.description: They’re neither Never Trumpers nor independents, but they’re increasingly meh on MAGA.

item.title: ‘Not a Perfect Union’: Neil Gorsuch on America at 250
item.description: David French and the Supreme Court justice discuss how the ideals of 1776 shaped — and strain — the country today.

item.title: John Roberts Believes in an America That Doesn’t Exist
item.description: Guess who benefits from a “colorblind Constitution.”

item.title: Neil deGrasse Tyson: Give Us the Aliens
item.description: Is that too much to ask for?

item.title: A Democrat Who Makes Me Listen
item.description: An interview with Representative Jake Auchincloss, a Democrat from the charismatic center.

item.title: Justice Neil Gorsuch on the ‘Miracle’ of Agreement on the Court
item.description: David French and the Supreme Court justice discuss how the ideals of 1776 shaped — and strain — the country today.

item.title: You Can’t Be Born Here. You Can Only Die.
item.description: The existential pain of losing birth services.

item.title: If Kids Need GLP-1s, We’ve Already Let Them Down
item.description: The debate over treatment in kids is missing a bigger problem.

item.title: We’ll Miss OPEC When It’s Gone
item.description: If OPEC can’t manage oil markets, the world is in for a wild ride.

item.title: My Father Wrote Letters to the Cuban Government. Here Is Mine.
item.description: Please do not take the fact that I am writing this letter to you as a defense of U.S. policy toward Cuba, much less a call for American intervention.

item.title: French Literature Is in an Uproar
item.description: Changes at a famous French publishing house tell us a lot about the world we live in now.

item.title: The Historical Roots of Postliberalism
item.description: Postliberalism may seem like a contemporary movement, with champions like Vice President JD Vance and Patrick Deneen. But the tradition of liberal skepticism reaches much further back. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the historian Helena Rosenblatt looks back to 19th-century England to explain.

item.title: What Is Liberal in the Liberal Arts?
item.description: What was a liberal arts education designed to offer? More than recent discourse might lead you to believe. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the historian Helena Rosenblatt explains why the liberal arts have strayed so far from their roots as a training ground for civic virtues and citizenry.

item.title: ‘When You Think of It, We Shouldn’t Even Have an Election’
item.description: Who will keep the 2026 ballots straight?

item.title: Why A.I. Will Never Replace Your Doctor
item.description: I use A.I. all the time to help me diagnose and treat my patients. I also know where it falls short.

item.title: Trump’s Ukraine Policy Is Succeeding While His Iran Policy Flails
item.description: With Ukraine, you can glimpse the shape of success.

item.title: Democrats, Keir Starmer Is a Warning
item.description: Keir Starmer’s collapse was written into the nature of his victory.

item.title: Big Payouts for Some Doctors, Too Little for Others
item.description: Readers discuss how arbitration has led to some huge awards while emergency care is often underpaid. Also: Don’t abandon America’s Afghan friends.

item.title: A Movie About Women Who Love Their Work? Groundbreaking.
item.description: It turns out that some women do, in fact, dream of labor, at least the kind that comes with a sense of mastery, agency and glamour.

item.title: Operation Epic Fury, Meet Operation Colossal Blunder
item.description: With the threat of a future closing of the Strait of Hormuz a drone strike away, the Iranians appear to actually hold the cards.

item.title: The Book That Changed How I Think About Liberalism
item.description: The historian Helena Rosenblatt traces the roots of liberalism and the values that many have forgotten.

item.title: The Book That Changed How I Think About Liberalism
item.description: The historian Helena Rosenblatt traces the roots of liberalism and the values that many have forgotten.

item.title: This Trump Proposal is Good for Private Equity — and Not Good for Our Retirement Accounts
item.description: A win for private equity. A loss for our retirement assets.

item.title: America Will Pay Dearly for Its Energy Arrogance
item.description: The idea that the U.S. is immune to the oil crisis is a mistaken one.

item.title: My Parents Are Old. They Need to Plan for It.
item.description: A few small changes to their home and habits will make it much more likely that they can age in their own home.

item.title: Speak, Yuppie
item.description: The young urban professionals of the 1980s changed our cities, our politics and our meritocratic work lives.

item.title: Disabled People Are Being Abandoned by the G.O.P.
item.description: George H.W. Bush showed how Republicans can be disabled Americans’ greatest allies. Why isn’t Donald Trump honoring his legacy?

item.title: If Democrats Have Appropriate Fear of Trump, They Will Elect Platner
item.description: Don’t let the Platner be the enemy of the good.

item.title: Reducing Highway Deaths Involving Trucks
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay faulting regulators. Also: The Democrats fight back.

item.title: Trump Shouldn’t Expect Much From His China Trip
item.description: China no longer looks to an erratic America as a model to emulate, but as a distraction to be managed.

item.title: This Is What Should Unite the Right and the Left on A.I.
item.description: Both parties can agree to do a whole lot on A.I., but they have to move faster.

item.title: Could California Really Elect a Republican Governor? This Race Has Become a Hot Mess.
item.description: Why is the country’s most populous state stuck with such a bad choice for governor?

item.title: In a Minneapolis Public School, a Cure for Toxic Politics
item.description: This school gave me hope for our democracy.

item.title: Why the A.I. Job Apocalypse (Probably) Won’t Happen
item.description: The feeling of efficiency should be mistrusted.

item.title: Posting the Ten Commandments in Classrooms
item.description: Readers discuss a Texas law requiring the display of the Ten Commandments in classrooms. Also: A job seeker’s tale; long movies.

item.title: What Would the Nation’s Founders Think of Trump? 250 Years Later, Here Is the Answer.
item.description: Jefferson’s quill pen drew blood.

item.title: How Lauren Sánchez Bezos Inspired ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’
item.description: The real-life Met Gala vs. the one onscreen.

item.title: America Is Officially an Empire in Decline
item.description: President Trump has overextended the empire dangerously.

item.title: It’s Only 5,000 Troops, but America Will Come to Regret Its Rash Withdrawal From Germany
item.description: This is more than just another bilateral spat.

item.title: It Just Got Harder to Get an Abortion for Every Woman in America
item.description: A ruling by a federal appeals court has blocked access to abortion pills via telemedicine.

item.title: Who Will Stand Up to the Supreme Court Justices?
item.description: Congress must once again defend democracy from a hostile court.

item.title: Slouching Toward Kamala Harris
item.description: The Democrats’ fundamental condition is a late-Trumpian stasis.

item.title: His Majesty and Our Travesty
item.description: The real king delivers a needed royal lesson on democracy.

item.title: What Is Higher Education For?
item.description: Readers respond to Bret Stephens’s column about the recent Yale report on reforming academia.

item.title: Rural America Is Getting Blindsided by Data and Detention Centers
item.description: Namely, immigrant detention centers and data centers.

item.title: The Supreme Court Steps Backward on Voting and Race
item.description: Readers discuss what one calls “the most recent Supreme Court assault on the Voting Rights Act of 1965.” Also: Shifts in drug policy.
